name: Apple Orchard Pie
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Cold water (possibly up to 4 tbsp)
1/2 cup Sugar (use confectioners sugar if apples are juicy)
recipeInstructions:
1. Nutmeg: In a bowl, combine the flour and salt. Cut in the shortening until the mixture resembles small peas.
2. Form dough: Sprinkle three to four tablespoons of cold water over the mixture, tossing lightly with a fork until the dough holds together. Shape into a ball.
3. Roll out: Dust a pastry cloth or board with flour. Roll the dough into a 10-inch circle and fit it loosely into an 8-inch pie pan. Fold the excess dough around the edge to form a rim, flute as desired. Prick the crust all over with a fork. Bake at 450°F for 10 to 12 minutes until lightly browned. Let cool.
4. Prepare filling: Peel and grate the cooking apples. Add the sugar (either type works) and nutmeg; mix well.
5. Assemble pie: Transfer the apple mixture into the baked crust.
6. Topping: Whip the cream until stiff peaks form, then fold in the confectioners sugar. Spread over the top of the filling.
7. Final topping: Sprinkle additional nutmeg on top for flavor.
Description: This recipe offers a unique fresh apple flavor without cooking the filling. Simply combine grated apples, sugar (or confectioners sugar), and nutmeg in a pie shell, then top with whipped cream. A favorite from the Junior Pillsbury bake off in 1951.
